records.Description:
Good evening,
We are not sure what is going on with this job on your side. We just received a cancellation notice for this job. We are a bit confused at this cancellation, as this job was completed on May 12, 2026 and submitted on May 14, 2026. You cannot cancel a job that was done more than a month ago. We will expect payment in full for this job in the amount of $285. We look forward to your reassurance of this. Thank you.

Subject: new vendor

My name is Eric Swan, and I am reaching out on behalf of Swans Construction LLC. We are interested in becoming a service vendor for Vixxo and would appreciate the opportunity to discuss how we can support your clients.
We provide a wide range of facility maintenance, repair, and construction services throughout Missouri, Illinois, Kansas, and surrounding areas. Our capabilities include:
• General Facility Maintenance
• Plumbing Repairs and Installations
• Electrical Repairs and Lighting Services
• HVAC Repairs and Preventive Maintenance
• Door, Lock, and Hardware Repairs
• Concrete and Asphalt Repairs
• Sealcoating, Crack Filling, and Striping
• Fence Repair and Installation
• Drywall, Painting, and Carpentry
• Ceiling Tile and Grid Repairs
• Power Washing and Exterior Cleaning
• Landscaping and Grounds Maintenance
• Irrigation Repairs
• Snow Removal and Ice Management
• Emergency and After-Hours Service
We are fully insured and equipped to handle both routine maintenance and emergency service requests. Our team is committed to providing quality workmanship, timely communication, and reliable service.
Please let me know what additional information is needed to complete the vendor onboarding process. We look forward to the opportunity to work with Vixxo and support your national accounts.
Thank you for your time and consideration.


Eric Swan
Swans Construction LLC
📞 314-852-4581
✉️ eric@swanllc.org<mailto:eric@swanllc.org>

My name is Tatev and I'm reaching out from RICO Electric to express our interest in becoming an approved electrical/lighting and EV charging contractor for Vixxo.
RICO Electric is a licensed Canadian electrical and lighting contractor specializing in commercial and multi-location environments.

What We Offer:

• 24/7 Emergency Repair: On-demand dispatch with licensed electricians and fully stocked service trucks

You don't often get email from tatevik.aleksanyan@ricoelectric.com. Learn why this is important<https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification>
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of Vixxo. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Hi Maria,

My name is Tatev and I'm reaching out from RICO Electric to express our interest in becoming an approved electrical/lighting and EV charging contractor for Vixxo.
RICO Electric is a licensed Canadian electrical and lighting contractor specializing in commercial and multi-location environments.

What We Offer:

• 24/7 Emergency Repair: On-demand dispatch with licensed electricians and fully stocked service trucks
• Lighting & General Electrical: LED retrofits, parking lot and canopy lighting, electrical repairs, and energy management
• Maintenance & ​ Programs: Scheduled inspections and preventative maintenance to maximize charger uptime and protect your investment
• EV Charging Station Installation: Full-scope installation and DC fast chargers at commercial and fuel retail sites

We'd welcome the opportunity to learn more about Vixxo's contractor qualification/onboarding  process and next steps.
I'd love to set up a quick 15-minute call to walk you through our full range of services. Just send over a day and time that works for you and I'll send the invite.

Best Regards,

Subject: New voicemail from Roberts Plumb via VENDOR RELATIONS

Attachment: voicemail_16084165646_06-26-2026_12-40-55.wav

Caller ID: Roberts Plumb

Callback number: 6084165646

Duration: 01:57



Hello, this is Haley. I'm calling from Robert's plumbing. I have been trying to get long story short. We don't have login information, so a username or password for the VIXO portal where we're supposed to submit the invoices. I've been trying to call and no one is picking up. I've tried accounting. We've emailed back and forth with the help desk and I keep being provided just the link for the login but we don't have any username or password information. We've never been provided that information so we don't have any of it. So we're really trying to be compliant because we have two outstanding work orders that we probably look like they're past due on your end. However, we've been trying to submit them and everybody that we've been sending them to just keep saying that we need to send it through the portal. So again, we're really trying to be compliant. We're really trying to get this information so that we can get everything uploaded appropriately so we can get everything done and processed within a timely manner. It is about quarter to one on Friday, June 26. We are only in the office until 4 p.m. Central Standard Time. Our phone number is 608-416-5646. And again, my name is Healey. I'm back in the office Mondays through Thursday this next week, 7.30 a.m. to 4 p.m. Central Standard Time, again phone number 608-416-5646. Really needing somebody to give us a call back so we can get this issue resolved as soon as possible. Thank you. Bye-bye.

records.Description:
The biggest thing is your entering of documents. First of all I entered an incorrect document on one SR and it would not let me remover. Now it is showing three delinquent invoices. RS 1-6578758122 will not let me do anything with it and it shows delinquent. I suppose the worst thing is when I have issues with the portal, there is no one to personally contact. To say the least it is a nightmare, the way it is laid out and the process of following through with it. My minimum is one hour at $110.00 per hour, and this site only wants to pay the time I was on site. You need to do away with the invoice processing on this site and go back to using the invoices I send in.

We are reaching out to inform you that we are updating our insurance requirements. As part of this adjustment, all providers are required to submit a new Certificate of Insurance (COI) – General Liability, Auto Liability, and Worker’s Compensation.


We kindly ask that you work with your insurance agent to ensure your updated COI includes all requirements as outlined on the new sample certificate. You can access sample cert below:

records.Subject: Certificate of Insurance
records.Description:
Please see attached Certificate of Insurance.  If you have questions regarding the content of this document, please refer to the Agency Contact information on this form and ask to speak with an associate regarding a Certificate of Insurance.  If this proof of insurance is no longer required, you can contact us and request to be removed as a certificate holder (certificates@imacorp.com).

Thank you,
IMA Certificate Team

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS:
Q: Why am I receiving this?
A: The Named Insured listed in the top left corner of the document is performing, or has performed, operations in which they were required to provide evidence insurance.

Q: What is a Certificate of Insurance?
A: A written document providing a summary of insurance coverage of the Named Insured listed in the top left corner of the document.

Q: What is this costing me?
A: Certificates are issued as a service to the Named Insured. There is no charge or cost to Certificate Holders.

Dear Team,

This is Summit HVAC Inc - 41710

We hope this message finds you well. To keep pace with inflation, we will
implement a rate adjustment effective July 1, 2026.

Our updated rates will be as follows:

Regular labor: $130 per hour

Refrigeration labor: $170 per hour

Please update the portal with our new rates.

We appreciate your business and look forward to continuing to provide you
with reliable HVAC and refrigeration services.


Thank you,
Aleksandra Chimova

1.  Certificate Holder Please update the certificate holder to: Vixxo Corporation 7580 N. Dobson Road, Suite 201 Scottsdale, AZ 85256 COI@vixxo.com<mailto:COI@vixxo.com>  CORRECT
  2.  Description of Operations – Required Wording The current language references “Vixxo Kansas, LLC,” which does not match our required wording. Please revise to: “Vixxo Corporation, its subsidiaries, affiliates, related entities and their officers, officials, employees, volunteers, its customers, the owner, operator and, if required, mortgagee of any site where the subcontractor performs services are included as Additional Insured with respect to General Liability per CG 20 10 & CG 20 37 (or equivalent), Automobile Liability per CA 20 48 (or equivalent), and as required by written agreement.”  CORRECTED
  3.  General Liability – Additional Insured Endorsements The policy currently includes Travelers’ proprietary Blanket Additional Insured endorsement (CG D6 04 02 19). Our requirements call for CG 20 10 (04/13) and CG 20 37 (04/13), or exact equivalents that provide both ongoing and completed operations coverage. Please provide the required ISO forms or confirm equivalent endorsements.   I will need to order these endorsements
  4.  Automobile Liability – Additional Insured Endorsement The COI states AI status for Auto Liability, but no Auto AI endorsement was provided. Our requirements call for CA 20 48 (10/13) or an exact equivalent. Please provide CA 20 48 or confirm an equivalent endorsement.  PAGE 11-15
  5.  Workers Compensation – Waiver of Subrogation The COI states that a waiver applies, but no WC waiver endorsement was attached. Please provide the appropriate waiver endorsement (e.g., WC 00 03 13 or equivalent). PAGE 10
30‑Day Notice of Cancellation The COI currently reflects only the standard ACORD language. Our agreement requires a guaranteed 30‑day notice of cancellation. Please add the appropriate endorsement or policy language supporting this requirement  WILL ORDER

I hope you’re doing well. I’m reaching out regarding the Certificate of Insurance issued for Mark One Electric Co., Inc. After reviewing the COI and the attached endorsements, we identified several items that need to be updated to meet our contractual insurance requirements.

Below is a summary of the corrections needed:


  1.  Certificate Holder Please update the certificate holder to: Vixxo Corporation 7580 N. Dobson Road, Suite 201 Scottsdale, AZ 85256 COI@vixxo.com<mailto:COI@vixxo.com>

  1.  Description of Operations – Required Wording The current language references “Vixxo Kansas, LLC,” which does not match our required wording. Please revise to: “Vixxo Corporation, its subsidiaries, affiliates, related entities and their officers, officials, employees, volunteers, its customers, the owner, operator and, if required, mortgagee of any site where the subcontractor performs services are included as Additional Insured with respect to General Liability per CG 20 10 & CG 20 37 (or equivalent), Automobile Liability per CA 20 48 (or equivalent), and as required by written agreement.”

  1.  General Liability – Additional Insured Endorsements The policy currently includes Travelers’ proprietary Blanket Additional Insured endorsement (CG D6 04 02 19). Our requirements call for CG 20 10 (04/13) and CG 20 37 (04/13), or exact equivalents that provide both ongoing and completed operations coverage. Please provide the required ISO forms or confirm equivalent endorsements.

  1.  Automobile Liability – Additional Insured Endorsement The COI states AI status for Auto Liability, but no Auto AI endorsement was provided. Our requirements call for CA 20 48 (10/13) or an exact equivalent. Please provide CA 20 48 or confirm an equivalent endorsement.

  1.  Workers Compensation – Waiver of Subrogation The COI states that a waiver applies, but no WC waiver endorsement was attached. Please provide the appropriate waiver endorsement (e.g., WC 00 03 13 or equivalent).

  1.  30‑Day Notice of Cancellation The COI currently reflects only the standard ACORD language. Our agreement requires a guaranteed 30‑day notice of cancellation. Please add the appropriate endorsement or policy language supporting this requirement.

Customer Name: Vixxo Corporation
Service Address: 2339 Volunteer Pkwy, Bristol, TN 37620

Services
5/11/26- Service Request #: 1-6562679592 NTE $400-- 2.5 hrs labor, 30 min travel, $50 travel fee-- Electrical repairs based on last service call.
All Labor Rates - Labor Hot Side
05/11
On arrival found outdoor outlet blown out picture is in app. Removed outlet and tested again it tripped again went back to panel tracked neutral down feeding circuit and found someone didn’t wire unit breaker they went straight to bus bar. Fixed that issue and breaker stopped tripping. We checked the outlets associated to ensure nothing else was wrong due to all the problems we have found so far across the building. We found the other three outlets were all wired backwards. Some hot to neutral, grounds not wired in and twisted around themselves. And outlets wired backwards with hots going to neutral side of outlet and neutral going to hot side of outlet. Fixed all of those reset breaker no trip occurred and we plugged all computers back in and tested to make sure they came on. Vixxo will call tomorrow and see if we have parts to return and replace the two outdoor outlets. Neither outlet is in use so not an emergency.

Thanks Dmitrii!
I don’t see you in the system, so here is everything we would need to get you onboarded.

Documents Required to be Added as a Supplier/Vendor Partner:

  *   Prospect form – Signed and dated
  *   W9 – Signed and dated from current year of Business type must be checked, and if LLC, please add to the classification
  *   ACH form – Signed and dated

     *   Account information must match the voided check or bank letter – double check to ensure all zero’s are added!


  *   Voided check or bank letter

     *   Voided check needs to have the account name, bank name, account number, and routing number; no handwritten information will be accepted
     *   Bank letter must be less than 1 year old and have the account name, bank name, account number, and routing number, on bank letterhead


  *   Certificate of insurance – see Vixxo COI Sample for reference o Commercial general liability, auto liability, workers’ compensation, and professional liability are required

     *   Vixxo must be certificate holder
     *   Vixxo must be additional insured on commercial general liability and auto liability
     *   Subrogation waiver on commercial general liability, auto liability, workers’ compensation, and professional liability
     *   The certificate of insurance must meet the minimum limit requirements as presented on the Vixxo COI Sample

Service Provider

Internal Employees

1. Performance Expectations & Service Standards

  *   What “first‑time fix” means and why it matters
  *   Expected arrival windows and communication standards
  *   How performance impacts customer satisfaction and future work

2. Mobile Compliance: What Counts and What Doesn’t

  *   What actions count toward compliance (SMS, IVR, VixxoLink)
  *   When to use each channel
  *   What should be reserved for emergencies

3. VixxoLink Overview

  *   How to navigate the platform
  *   Key features that support faster job completion
  *   Common mistakes and how to avoid them

4. SMS Process Overview

  *   When SMS is the fastest option
  *   How to ensure messages are logged correctly
  *   Examples of effective SMS communication

5. IVR Phone Call Best Practices

  *   When IVR is the right tool
  *   How to use it efficiently
  *   Ensuring accurate updates

6. Why Calling the Project Manager Can Slow You Down

  *   How direct calls bypass tracking and delay approvals
  *   When escalation is appropriate
  *   Alternatives that keep the workflow moving

7. Need Help? Support Resources

  *   Who to contact for what
  *   Hours of availability
  *   How to get quick answers without disrupting workflow



1. Setting and Reinforcing Performance Expectations

  *   How to communicate expectations clearly
  *   Monitoring first‑time fix rates and arrival times
  *   Coaching providers on improvement areas

2. Ensuring Provider Compliance

  *   How to track compliance across SMS, IVR, and VixxoLink
  *   Identifying patterns of non‑compliance
  *   Strategies to improve adoption

3. Internal Use of VixxoLink

  *   How internal teams can use the platform to reduce friction
  *   Ensuring data accuracy for reporting
  *   Common internal errors and how to prevent them

4. Understanding Communication Channels

  *   When to direct providers to SMS, IVR, or VixxoLink
  *   How to avoid unnecessary phone calls
  *   Escalation paths and when they should be used

5. Reducing Bottlenecks

  *   Improving response times and approvals
  *   Streamlining handoffs between teams

6. Supporting Providers Without Creating Workarounds

  *   How to help without bypassing systems
  *   Reinforcing the use of official channels
  *   Training providers on self‑service tools

7. Need Help?

  *   How to engage SPM
  *   Where to take that AP question/concern
  *   Where to take that AR question/concern

Good morning!

I hope you're doing well. I'm reaching out regarding the Certificates of Insurance we received for Advanced Mechanical Systems Inc. After reviewing the policy documents, it appears that a few required endorsements are missing for compliance with our agreement.
At this time, we were unable to locate the following General Liability endorsements:

  *   CG 20 10 (or equivalent)

  *   CG 20 37 (or equivalent)

These endorsements are required to provide Additional Insured status for both ongoing and completed operations. The current policy includes a blanket additional insured provision, but it does not extend to completed operations, which is a requirement.

When available, please forward the applicable endorsements, or let me know if I should reach out to the carrier directly.

Thank you for your help, and please feel free to contact me if you need any clarification.

records.Subject: Evansville Handyman Insurance Changes 7/1/26
records.Description:
*Evansville Handyman LLC – 2026 Insurance Changes Effective July 1, 2026*

Dear Valued Client,

I am writing to provide an important update regarding Evansville Handyman
LLC’s insurance coverage. Beginning *July 1, 2026*, we have updated our
policy structure to reflect the current status of our business operations.

Evansville Handyman LLC is now operated solely by the owner, with no
employees or subcontracted labor. We have not had any employees for over a
year. Because of this, we have formally discontinued our *Workers’
Compensation Insurance*, as it is no longer applicable to a single‑operator
business.

Additionally, we have removed *Commercial Auto Liability* coverage from our
policy. All business transportation is handled personally by the owner, and
no vehicles are operated by employees or additional drivers.

Our *General Liability* policy remains active and in good standing, and we
have added *Umbrella* and *Errors & Omissions (E&O)* coverage to strengthen
our protection and maintain compliance with most vendor requirements.
Updated Certificates of Insurance are available upon request or through
your vendor portal, if applicable.

If your organization requires Workers’ Compensation or Commercial Auto
Liability coverage even for sole‑operator businesses with no employees,
please notify us. We will need clarification on your policy requirements
and may need to reevaluate our participation in your vendor network if
these coverages are mandatory.

We appreciate your partnership and will continue to provide reliable
service under our updated coverage structure. Please let us know if any
additional documentation or clarification is needed.

Sincerely, *Brian Turner* Owner / Operator Evansville Handyman LLC

records.Subject: Re: Vixxo Monthly Business Summary (-) - June 2026
records.Description:
Hello,

I am just writing to ask to be removed from this monthly business summary.

Sadly we do not work for Vixxo anymore. We worked for Vixxo servicing Starbucks for many years. I provided excellent service for 8 years or so and we were unable to negotiate our contract to continue working for Vixxo.

It’s sad really but is how it goes when upper management in companies change people.

Thank you and have a great day!

Bradley

On Jul 1, 2026, at 8:59 AM, Service.ProviderManagement@vixxo.com wrote:

﻿

Valued Service Provider:

At Vixxo, we consider Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to be an essential component of world-class customer service. That's why we place a high value on providers who understand how we measure our mutual success.

Understanding the attached KPI Report

KPIs serve as strategic markers to ensure we not only provide best in class service to our clients, but also have visibility into operational changes and trends. Attached is a one-page summary that gives you an overview of your monthly activity with Vixxo. Each month, we will send you an updated look at your team's performance.

Report Definitions (Service Provider targets are in parentheses)

Response SLA (90%): Arriving on site within the SLA established per the client requirement and work order priority
Completion SLA (90%): Completing the job within the SLA established per the client requirement and work order priority
First Time Fix (90%): Completing the work order the first time/day on site
Callback / Recall (15%): Percentage of time multiple dispatches are required for the same work order

If you are not the right person to receive this report, kindly let us know what email address you would prefer this information be sent to. Send a request to Service.ProviderManagement@vixxo.com.

Some Quick Reminders for Vixxo Service Requests

ACH Direct Deposit vs. Checks. If you are still receiving paper checks from Vixxo, you can get paid quicker by signing up for ACH deposit. With ACH, your payments are directly deposited into your bank account which allows you to receive them both faster and more securely. Visit the Vixxo Help Center to get started
Vixxo  Technology (VixxoLink  or MyFSN) vs. Phoning in ETAs and Updates. Whenever possible, use VixxoLink or MyFSN  to update ETAs and notes for your work orders

If there are extenuating circumstances and you're not able to use VixxoLink or MyFSN for day-to-day service request communication and updates, please use our phone system to provide updates

Please listen carefully to the options before requesting a team member, as our automated phone system can handle several Service Requests updates by simply pressing keys on your phone


In the field, Technicians should be using Vixxo's mobile technology when timing in and out of client jobs. Technicians also have the ability update ETAs and add notes to Service Requests using our mobile technology

Quotes. Please remember that all quotes need  to be entered through the service provider portal in MyFSN or VixxoLink. If you need a refresher on the steps to take to create a quote, visit the Vixxo Help Center

To accelerate turnaround times in the review and approval process, submit all quotes within 48 hours

Invoices. To avoid rejected invoices, always:

Submit invoices within 15 days of completing work
Submit labor charges that match time on-site (confirm time-in and time-out)
Submit receipts for parts over $400
If you have a problem with an invoice, contact our AP Hotline, either by creating a ticket in the Vixxo Help Center or by calling 877-860-7398

NTE Increases. If an NTE increase is needed, call us while your tech is still on-site and before proceeding with the work
Service Provider Support Matters. For items such as system training, system access or other support questions, use the Vixxo Help Center or contact the Service Provider Hotline, either by e-mail or by calling 866-988-5376

To discuss any concerns about your service provider account with Vixxo, including administrative support, contact your dedicated Service Provider Manager


It takes a team to be successful. We genuinely value and appreciate your commitment to serving our esteemed clients.

Thank You,

Team Vixxo

> make sure these are flowing through properly. Below I provided a brief
> description of each of these statuses
>
>

>    usually involve an SR not being set up properly, so calcualtions are not
>    updating as expected.

>    though, such as making sure the invoce is below the set not to exceed. any
>    failure will route to this queue for our AP team to review.

>    to if they require additional verification
>    - Billing Specialist Review- This means the invoice has been
>    approved/validated by the AP/OPS team and the billing dept is preparin to

records.Subject: Reminder: Invoice 6287489 is due from Spears Services, Inc. - $331.00
records.Description:
Your invoice from Spears Services, Inc.

Hi ,

This is a friendly reminder from Spears Services, Inc. that invoice 6287489 for $331.00 is due. Please see the attached invoice to review and pay.

Job Number: #6287489
Service Date:  May 05, 2026

Customer Name: Vixxo Corporation
Service Address: 2521 N Roan St, Johnson City, TN 37601

Services
WO# 349622424-- NTE: $350 Store has no power possible tripped breaker
Misc Charges - Labor Hot side OT
05/03
Arrived at 2:30 pm. I knocked on the front door but it's locked and it says they are closed due to Internet issues the lights are on and they have power. I knocked a few times and left. Will return later.

05/03
Arrived at 5 pm. The guy onsite said the Internet runs everything in their store, their phones and their cash registers and such, and it was all down. I went to the control cabinet and the modem was online, but the router was off. I found the power wire and when I plugged it in it still wasn't coming on, so I tracked it back to the power converter and that was extremely loose and when I plugged it in all in and rebooted the modem and then turned the router back on it bring the phones back and wifi came on. The only thing not back online was the cash registers which we're stuck from trying to boot without Internet. I told him to close them out and reboot them and it should be fine, if not he will have to call their inhouse IT department which he said they work on their equipment remotely, but have to have the Internet to do so. Job Completed.

Materials
Truck Charges - Johnson City

Services subtotal  $231.00
Materials subtotal  $100.00
Subtotal  $331.00
A-TN Sales Tax  $0.00

Total job price  $331.00
Amount Due  $331.00

Pay



Thank you for the opportunity to be of service.
If you wish to pay on line, go to https:///www.spearsservices.com and click on Pay tab.

Your invoice from Spears Services, Inc.

Hi ,

This is a friendly reminder from Spears Services, Inc. that invoice 6287489 for $331.00 is due. Please see the attached invoice to review and pay.

Job Number: #6287489
Service Date:  May 05, 2026

Customer Name: Vixxo Corporation
Service Address: 2521 N Roan St, Johnson City, TN 37601

Services
WO# 349622424-- NTE: $350 Store has no power possible tripped breaker
Misc Charges - Labor Hot side OT
05/03
Arrived at 2:30 pm. I knocked on the front door but it's locked and it says they are closed due to Internet issues the lights are on and they have power. I knocked a few times and left. Will return later.

05/03
Arrived at 5 pm. The guy onsite said the Internet runs everything in their store, their phones and their cash registers and such, and it was all down. I went to the control cabinet and the modem was online, but the router was off. I found the power wire and when I plugged it in it still wasn't coming on, so I tracked it back to the power converter and that was extremely loose and when I plugged it in all in and rebooted the modem and then turned the router back on it bring the phones back and wifi came on. The only thing not back online was the cash registers which we're stuck from trying to boot without Internet. I told him to close them out and reboot them and it should be fine, if not he will have to call their inhouse IT department which he said they work on their equipment remotely, but have to have the Internet to do so. Job Completed.

    On Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 06:03:02 AM PDT, Service.ProviderManagement@vixxo.com <service.providermanagement@vixxo.com> wrote:   

 
Valued Service Provider: 

At Vixxo, we consider Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to be an essential component of world-class customer service. That's why we place a high value on providers who understand how we measure our mutual success. 

 Understanding the attached KPI Report

KPIs serve as strategic markers to ensure we not only provide best in class service to our clients, but also have visibility into operational changes and trends. Attached is a one-page summary that gives you an overview of your monthly activity with Vixxo. Each month, we will send you an updated look at your team's performance. 

Report Definitions (Service Provider targets are in parentheses)
   
   - Response SLA (90%): Arriving on site within the SLA established per the client requirement and work order priority
   - Completion SLA (90%): Completing the job within the SLA established per the client requirement and work order priority 
   - First Time Fix (90%): Completing the work order the first time/day on site
   - Callback / Recall (15%): Percentage of time multiple dispatches are required for the same work order

If you are not the right person to receive this report, kindly let us know what email address you would prefer this information be sent to. Send a request to Service.ProviderManagement@vixxo.com.

Some Quick Reminders for Vixxo Service Requests
    
   - ACH Direct Deposit vs. Checks. If you are still receiving paper checks from Vixxo, you can get paid quicker by signing up for ACH deposit. With ACH, your payments are directly deposited into your bank account which allows you to receive them both faster and more securely. Visit the Vixxo Help Center to get started
   - Vixxo Technology (VixxoLink or MyFSN) vs. Phoning in ETAs and Updates. Whenever possible, use VixxoLink or MyFSN to update ETAs and notes for your work orders       
      - If there are extenuating circumstances and you're not able to use VixxoLink or MyFSN for day-to-day service request communication and updates, please use our phone system to provide updates          
         - Please listen carefully to the options before requesting a team member, as our automated phone system can handle several Service Requests updates by simply pressing keys on your phone 
 
       
      - In the field, Technicians should be using Vixxo's mobile technology when timing in and out of client jobs. Technicians also have the ability update ETAs and add notes to Service Requests using our mobile technology

   - Quotes. Please remember that all quotes need to be entered through the service provider portal in MyFSN or VixxoLink. If you need a refresher on the steps to take to create a quote, visit the Vixxo Help Center      
      - To accelerate turnaround times in the review and approval process, submit all quotes within 48 hours
 
   - Invoices. To avoid rejected invoices, always:      
      - Submit invoices within 15 days of completing work
      - Submit labor charges that match time on-site (confirm time-in and time-out)
      - Submit receipts for parts over $400
      - If you have a problem with an invoice, contact our AP Hotline, either by creating a ticket in the Vixxo Help Center or by calling 877-860-7398
 
   - NTE Increases. If an NTE increase is needed, call us while your tech is still on-site and before proceeding with the work
   -  Service Provider Support Matters. For items such as system training, system access or other support questions, use the Vixxo Help Center or contact the Service Provider Hotline, either by e-mail or by calling 866-988-5376       
      - To discuss any concerns about your service provider account with Vixxo, including administrative support, contact your dedicated Service Provider Manager
 
 
It takes a team to be successful. We genuinely value and appreciate your commitment to serving our esteemed clients.
 
Thank You,

Team Vixxo

NEW Tool Available: Instant Certificate Validation

The Instant Certificate Validation Report is a tool provided by Applied Systems Inc., at the request of the broker, that can flag any reductions in coverage or changes to insured status occurring after the certificate of insurance was issued.

This tool is meant to help you, as the certificate holder, monitor changes to this certificate. It is not a replacement for the certificate of insurance, which can be found attached to this email.
View the Report for this Certificate
Note: The report link is associated with certificate number #1554684708 and will only provide updates for that certificate for so long as the insured provides access.


How To Use

  1. Open the Instant Certificate Validation Report or scan the QR code.
  2. Review the report to ensure no changes were made to the policies, coverages, or insured status. This report will only highlight reductions of coverage.
  3. Follow the prompt at the top of the report for next steps, if necessary.


By accessing this report, you acknowledge and agree that the information conveyed to you is based on information provided by your broker of record and Applied is not responsible for the completeness, accuracy, legality, quality, and suitability of the information provided.
Use of the Instant Certificate Validation Report is subject to the  Applied Systems Inc. Privacy Policy.
